body:
Use network policies to isolate traffic in your cluster network.

**Rationale**

Running different applications on the same Kubernetes cluster creates a risk of one compromised application attacking a neighboring application. Network segmentation is important to ensure that containers can communicate only with those they are supposed to. A network policy is a specification of how selections of pods are allowed to communicate with each other and other network endpoints.

Network Policies are namespace scoped. When a network policy is introduced to a given namespace, all traffic not allowed by the policy is denied. However, if there are no network policies in a namespace all traffic will be allowed into and out of the pods in that namespace.

**Impact**

Once network policies are in use within a given namespace, traffic not explicitly allowed by a network policy will be denied. As such it is important to ensure that, when introducing network policies, legitimate traffic is not blocked.

**Audit**

Run the below command and review the NetworkPolicy objects created in the cluster.

```bash
kubectl get networkpolicy --all-namespaces
```



Ensure that each namespace defined in the cluster has at least one Network Policy.
name: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
remediationDescription:
### Kubernetes

1. For each affected namespace, identify the expected ingress and egress paths for applications running there.
2. Create a default deny NetworkPolicy for ingress, egress, or both, depending on the namespace baseline.
3. Add explicit allow policies for required traffic such as application frontends, service-to-service calls, DNS, monitoring, and ingress controllers.
4. Apply policies first in lower-risk namespaces and verify application connectivity.
5. Roll the pattern into Helm charts, Kustomize overlays, or namespace templates so new namespaces are created with policies.
6. Confirm the cluster CNI enforces NetworkPolicy; otherwise the objects may exist but not take effect.

Reference: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/network-policies/
description: All Namespaces without network Policies defined
